title: lamium maculatum f. glabratum
intro: Lamium maculatum f. glabratum, also known as spotted dead nettle, is a herbaceous perennial plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a sterile cultivar of the common spotted dead nettle, which has been bred to be seedless and has lost its toxic properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, and improving sleep quality. The herbal preparations of Lamium maculatum f. glabratum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a calming agent or used topically to soothe skin irritations.
